news.t: Australian lawmakers, grilling KPMG, suggest more regulation of audit industry may be needed

holdings.infoBox: EWA is an equity ETF with a total of 54 individual holdings. The top holdings are BHP Group stock at 16.09%, Commonwealth Bank of Australia at 13.22%, Westpac Banking at 5.87%, National Australia Bank at 5.58%, and ANZ Group Holdings at 5.10%.

overview.description: The iShares MSCI Australia ETF (EWA) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the MSCI Australia index, a market-cap-weighted index of Australian stocks. EWA was launched on Mar 12, 1996 and is issued by BlackRock.

data.title: Australia's first-quarter economic growth misses estimates on severe weather, weak demand
data.text: Australia's GDP expanded 2.5% in the first three months this year, missing expectations. On a quarter-on-quarter basis, Australia's GDP grew 0.3% compared with 0.5% forecast.

data.title: Australia Rate Increases Stay in Play Despite Easing Inflation
data.text: Australia's consumer-price growth slowed, but stayed high enough to keep the door open to further rate increases by the central bank.

data.title: ASX 200 index on edge as Australian bond yields fall after inflation cools
data.text: The ASX 200 Index remained in a tight range while Australian bond yields fell after the latest consumer inflation report. It was trading at A$8,660, a few points below this week's high of A$8,715.

data.title: Australian regulator flags rising global private credit risks, steps up oversight
data.text: Australia's financial regulator warned on Thursday that domestic private credit institutions are exposed to risks linked to global market developments ​and will require closer scrutiny.

data.title: Australia's tax changes will re-wire investors to chase income
data.text: Australia's planned tax overhaul is set to reshape the market's investment ​landscape, with high-dividend blue chips poised to benefit at the expense of growth-oriented stocks, fund managers say.

data.title: Australia's farmers, hit by Iran war costs and dry weather, grow less wheat
data.text: Justin Everitt is planting 50% less wheat this year than he thought he would.

data.text: Dubai is trying to get its air travel industry back on its feet. Dubai Airports said it was ramping up flights now that the UAE airspace is "fully restored.

data.title: Australia's Consumer Inflation Jumps on Oil Shock
data.text: Australia's consumer prices jumped in the first quarter, hitting the highest level since September 2023, as the impact of the energy shock fed into the data.
